transcript: Yeah, I was trying to make my way up 'ere.
Yeah, I have—You need to give me a cross street or something. I have no i—I've asked four people where you are. I don't know where you are. [laughs]
I b—I was holdin' for someone to get on the line and it went silent.
Yeah, I had you on hold. Um, can you give me cross streets? Are you in the mountains? Are you downtown Denver? Where you at?
Yeah, the line fell silent, there. I apologize. Um—
No, that's fine. I just need to know where you are so I can kinda help you out here, 'cause I have four different people; no one knows what you're talking about. ''[laughs].
Oh, you hit it smack dab on the head. Um, I live across the Great Divide. You know, I've got the four acres and I'm in the fork.
Okay. Are you trying to get to Denver?
I'm trying to get to Twist & Shout
Well, I've got the, ah, two large creeks runnin' through the middle of my back yard—
—Yeah, that's not—that doesn't help me. I need something else. You need to give me the name of the city that you're in or something.
’Cause I can't help you unless I know where you are.
The campfire pits, I've got 'em on either side.
I don't—That doesn't help me. You've gotta give me the name of the town you're in. It's just a campfire pit? That doesn't help me. 'Cause the next thing I'm gonna suggest is Google Maps.
Well, I think you've hit it smack dab on the head.
For the third time, yeah, yeah.
Well, what about the light rail? I mean, that shows some real initiative on my part, don't you think?

Yes, it does. You could take the light rail to either LoDo or you could take it to—You can take it to LoDo and you can take the 15 down. That's one way to do it, but other than that, I—I really don't know where you are, man.
Are you talkin' 'bout the real estate market, that I get to see everything on the way? 'Cause it's hot right now. Is that what you're saying?
Not at all. I don't think we understand each other at all, man.
No, no, no, no. You're just adding an addendum to my original question, is what you're kinda doing, right?
Not at all, man. I'm just tryin' to help you and I'm confused as hell right now, so—
—you can try to take the light rail to the bus station; that's one way to do it.
Okay, well, I think I'm showing initiative, here!
That's great! And I'll see you when you get here!
Okay, so when I start out, which direction do I start out?
Depends on where you're startin' from, right?.
[animated] Yeah, I know, right?! So, um, you could either try Google Maps at this point or you could check your phone, 'cause I don’t know where you're startin' at.
One thing you didn't mention, though, is how all the street signs are at the far side at the intersections, meaning you only discover the street you're looking for after it's kinda too late, y'know?
That's okay, yeah! But I'll see you when ya get here, man!
Are you on a service road, w—?
We're on the largest street in the United States [chuckling], the one that goes the longest from east to west. Ya can't miss it, man!
My point is there's no way to turn around for at least a good three to five miles, once you start out. And there—service roads don't exist, so it's—
—Oh, okay, well I—I don't know that, man.
—Look, I gotta go. I'm the only one here, so good luck, man!
If something's blocking the road, I'm sunk! Y'know what I'm saying?
Yeah, you are, and I don't know how to help that, man—
—I'll see you when you get here! Thanks—
